programme.title: Aussie Gold Hunters
programme.desc: The Poseidon Crew recruit a 14-year-old worker to help speed up their operation. Meanwhile, the Gold Timers are slowed down by a freak dust storm, which they will need to wait out.

programme.title: Aussie Gold Hunters
programme.desc: An angry confrontation could see Marcus, Linden and Dale thrown off their lease. Impurities in their gold force the Dust Devils to take a big gamble in search of making a healthy profit.

programme.title: Extreme Cheapskates
programme.desc: Meet extreme cheapskates the O'Brien's who manage to save big by refusing to buy anything new and instead find ingenious ways of fixing their broken belongings.

programme.title: Extreme Cheapskates
programme.desc: Stephanie's cheap behavior embarrasses her boyfriend, and Larry lives and works in his Texas garage.

programme.title: Extreme Cheapskates
programme.desc: A look back at the most extreme moments from the series, and exploring how far some individuals will go to scrimp on everyday purchases.

programme.title: Paranormal Caught on Camera
programme.sub-title: Goblin Road Crossing in Mexico and More
programme.desc: Spirits haunt the basement of an abandoned winery; shadow figures catch a flick at a decades-old movie theatre; a legendary creature is caught crossing the road.

programme.title: Kindred Spirits
programme.desc: Amy, Adam and Chip investigate the infamous Harrisville Farmhouse; they call upon the entire Perron family to help expose the truth behind the horrific haunting.

programme.title: Kindred Spirits
programme.desc: Answering the call of a couple suffering from paranormal attacks; the wife fears an entity is possessing her husband, causing blackouts and fits of anger.

programme.title: Body Bizarre
programme.desc: Italian professor Letizia feels no physical pain and has broken limbs with no discomfort. Filipino twins Charina and Chiara, 7, were born joined at the head.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Charlie, who's 25, is searching for his perfect `unicorn' to fit his `hot, crazy matrix', and public relations whizz Lorna seems to tick all of Charlie's boxes, but her love life has yet to match the success of her corporate career.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: It's the sixth dinner party, and it turns out textgate still has plenty of legs - and venom - left in it yet. Bec has a foolproof plan to defuse the tenson: an apology. But apparently that involves shifting the blame onto someone else.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics have been watching `Eurovision Song Contest', `Citadel', `The Greatest Auction', `Family Fortunes', `Open House: The Great Sex Experiment', `I Kissed a Boy', `The Big Celebrity Detox' and ITV News.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.desc: Principal Skinner announces that Springfield Elementary's two fourth grade classes will merge until the absent Mrs Krabappel returns. As a result, Bart reluctantly shares a desk with Nikki and develops a flirtatious rapport.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After erecting a wind turbine in the backyard, Homer realises some power is being directed to the local electric company, prompting him to remove his home from the grid and become completely dependent on an unreliable power source.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The authorities suspend civil liberties when radiation is released into the city, while Lisa dyes her hair a darker colour after growing tired of being blonde.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: This time the celebs watched 'Casualty', 'The 1% Club for Soccer Aid', 'Clarkson's Farm', 'Heretic', 'The Yorkshire Vet: At Home with the Greens', 'This Morning' and 'ITV News (Trump v Musk)'.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: This week, the Goggleboxers have been watching 'Britain's Got Talent', 'Married at First Sight Australia', 'Chess Masters: The Endgame', 'The Au Pair', 'This Morning', 'The Americas', 'Inside the Four Seasons Park Lane' and 'With Love, Meghan'.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: This time on Gogglebox it's 'The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up To Cancer', 'With Love', 'Meghan', 'Tempting Fortune', 'Fear', 'Bradley Walsh: Egypt's Cosmic Code', 'Last One Laughing', 'Jeopardy' and 'This Morning (toenails)'.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Uzzi rids Stefen of a terrible tribute to brotherly love and Alice lights up Amanda's life. Pash blows away Liam's trumpy tattoo and on top of all that, Sketch faces a not-so-tasty treat.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Sarah Millican gets her hands in some cottage pie, Fern Brady becomes Brandy Alexander, Munya Chawawa demonstrates a mastery of martial arts, John Kearns survives an operation, and Dara Ó Briain carefully selects a luggage trolley.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of some of the very best bits from Cats Does Countdown, featuring Lee Mack, Tom Allen, Judi Love, Richard Ayoade, Alan Carr, Jonathan Ross, Sarah Millican and many more.

programme.title: Channel Off Air
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: Channel Off Air
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: Platinum-selling, Grammy-nominated singer/songwriter, actor and author Jewel goes undercover to meet talented musicians and artists struggling to break through.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: Stephanie McMahon, chief brand officer of the WWE, goes undercover to find new talent for the WWE.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: A couple and their three daughters, having outgrown their home, look to move into a bigger space - but mum and dad have different priorities.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in Oxfordshire is amateur MC James, and his more sensible half Heather. Day two sees Marjie and Matt push the boat out with a Titanic-themed evening. Finally, Tutty and Sonja try to impress with their refined countryside dining.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Extrovert Jojo and long-term partner Adrian kick off the competition with their 1970s inspired menu. Day two sees young, fun-loving Mark and Zoe plan an international menu, and last to host are Caz and Dave with their Jewish-inspired family menu.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Former soldier Jason takes command of his military wedding to his future bride-to-be, Sacha. Having served in the army for 7 years, Jason wants to celebrate the heroes of WW2 by getting Sacha to arrive to her wedding in an old war plane, then a tank.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Movie Premiere
programme.desc: Jacquie feels self-conscious about her looks and is dreaming of a small, elegant traditional wedding. Her fiance Darren intends on making her his leading lady by throwing her into the spotlight with an amazing movie premier inspired wedding.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: TGI Fridays opened in 1965 and became one of the world's biggest restaurant chains. CEO Ray Blanchette wants to re-energise the brand, and goes undercover to see what staff think.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Club Med Europe Africa Middle East CEO Xavier Mufraggi goes undercover at his holiday parks.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Eagle Rock California, to visit Capri - a restaurant in a rut - owned by overgrown identical twins, Jim and Jeff. The twins could have walked straight out of Alice in Wonderland and their method of running a restaurant is just as fantastic. Half comedy act half cry-babies, Jim and Jeff seem oblivious to the grime and dust riddling their restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants, Mojito, The Junction (formerly known as Flamingos) and Bazzini, to check in with the owners he helped. Will he be pleased with their progress?

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.sub-title: Letting Go Is Hard to Do
programme.desc: With one kid already in college, Adina and Trey are looking to downsize. They are eager to sell fast before a subdivision floods the neighbourhood with a thousand brand-new homes, but their outdated property is making a lousy first impression.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The co-owners of South Brooklyn Foundry don't see eye-to-eye, causing communication to grow toxic while the restaurant falls into more debt; Gordon Ramsay steps in to reassess the Brooklyn eatery's priorities and straighten out the finances.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain finds himself baffled by a quantity of milk, John Kearns does some elaborate skipping, Munya Chawawa does some elaborate jumping, and Fern Brady walks too slowly. Amid the carnage, Little Alex Horne finds himself largely ignored.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson once again take on the famous words and numbers quiz. Jimmy Carr hosts proceedings, and Sean Lock faces Jon Richardson in a rematch of the original encounter. David O'Doherty is in Dictionary Corner along with Countdown regular Susie Dent. Meanwhile the country's favourite boffin Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from comedian Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Rhod Gilbert take on Lee Mack and Rob Beckett. Tim Key is in `Dictionary Corner' with Susie Dent, and Rachel Riley is assisted by Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: The team face their first test during Reynholm Industries' so-called `War on Stress'. The office is on fire, Roy has a sudden attack of kleptomania, and Jen's new shoes are two sizes too small.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Jen has a new admirer, but his romantic gesture goes horribly wrong when Jen's inability to tell the truth results in a very public humiliation. Meanwhile, Moss and Roy conduct an experiment with a `lonely hearts' website.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Sketch gives Andy a hand with his disastrous digits, while Alice designs popstar Michelle Heaton something a little special. Also, Pash rids Carly of her cracking calamity and Uzzi works on Ian's booby blunder.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Sketch gives Andy a hand with his disastrous digits, while Alice designs popstar Michelle Heaton something a little special. Also, Pash rids Carly of her cracking calamity and Uzzi works on Ian's booby blunder.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain finds himself baffled by a quantity of milk, John Kearns does some elaborate skipping, Munya Chawawa does some elaborate jumping, and Fern Brady walks too slowly. Amid the carnage, Little Alex Horne finds himself largely ignored.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson once again take on the famous words and numbers quiz. Jimmy Carr hosts proceedings, and Sean Lock faces Jon Richardson in a rematch of the original encounter. David O'Doherty is in Dictionary Corner along with Countdown regular Susie Dent. Meanwhile the country's favourite boffin Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from comedian Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: Channel Off Air
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: Channel Off Air
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, Carl and his German wife Anja serve up a meal inspired by Anja's homeland, while Leroy and Hayley cook Caribbean. However, Sangeeta and Sreekanth try to dance their way to the prize.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bedfordshire, Tom and Naomi prepare posh nosh, Victoria and Elliot serve a controversial goat curry and Neil and Charlie plan to impress their guests with mystery and magic.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Lee has just three weeks and £12,000 to pull off the perfect day for his bride-to-be Ellie. Lee wants a huge wedding that celebrates his dwarfism, and shows off his sense of humour. However, all Ellie wants is a low key, elegant and intimate wedding.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: All Hannah wants is a luxurious and relaxing wedding day where she looks a million dollars, but Tommy has a big romantic plan up his sleeve for his ex-water-skiing champ bride.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Petrified Bride
programme.desc: The sky's the limit for adrenaline junkie Jai as he plans on having a wedding in the sky, neglecting the fact that his bride-to-be Leanne is terrified of heights.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Party animal Harry has three weeks and £12,000 to pull off a day to impress his perfectionist bride-to-be Rosa, but while she dreams of a stylish, vintage English wedding that's all about romance and tradition. Harry, however, would like a fun full-scale Bollywood-inspired extravaganza.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Fashion Show
programme.desc: Jenna dreams of a traditional and timeless wedding that will never go out of style, James wants a cutting-edge, catwalk fashion show ceremony full of the latest trends.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Steven Newman, CEO of Loehmann's department stores, goes undercover posing as an aspiring entrepreneur in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to start up his own firm.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jane Grote Abell, chairwoman of Donatos Pizza and daughter of the company's founder, goes undercover posing as a contestant on a reality TV show who is trying to win money to open up her own restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants, Mojito, The Junction (formerly known as Flamingos) and Bazzini, to check in with the owners he helped. Will he be pleased with their progress?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The opening episode of a new series sees Gordon in Plainfield, New Jersey, trying to save a floundering soul food restaurant called Blackberry's. He comes up against the indomitable Shelley, an owner who runs her establishment with an iron fist, and doesn't want to hear what Gordon has to say. Gordon must find a way to convince her to change her ways if the restaurant is going to stand a chance.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States, travelling to New Jersey to try to snatch Italian restaurant Leone's from the jaws of failure. Owner Leone had a thriving business, but after a sudden illness she spent two years in hospital in a coma. When she returned, she was heartbroken to discover that her lazy son Michael had run all her efforts and the restaurant into the ground.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of an explosive two-part special, Gordon tries to turn around the fortunes of Los Angeles restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. It's only been open for 16 months but has already changed its menu more than 10 times, as well as going through 10 chefs and 10 waitresses, and owner Alan has spent his son's inheritance on the restaurant without permission, leading to much family conflict.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The explosive drama continues as Gordon is still in Los Angeles struggling to bring together the fragmented Saffron family and their failing restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Last time, son Daniel nearly came to blows with the head chef, and Gordon was shocked to learn that dad Alan had taken from Daniel's inheritance, without his knowledge, to help fund the start-up of the restaurant.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host the comedically contorted Countdown mash-up as Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Rob Brydon and Joe Wilkinson, with Huge Davies in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Bewilderness
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Bill Bailey performs live at the Grand Theatre in Swansea and delivers hilariously unfocused and scattered comedy styles, as well as his enviable musical skills.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: It's July 12th and the Quinn family want to get out of Derry before the Orange Parades start. Aunt Sarah's tarot cards predict a disaster for this trip - and she might be right.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin is over the moon when she becomes editor of the school magazine. Orla's obsessed by step aerobics and Da Gerry is in trouble with Ma Mary, and there's romance for Aunt Sarah.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Stand-up comic and presenter Jimmy Carr is at the helm as comedians Sean Lock and Jon Richardson head up the teams for Christmas capers. Guests featuring in the teams for some yuletide fun include Sarah Millican, Stephen Mangan and Joe Wilkinson. With lots of Christmas-themed questions and a sprinkling of tinsel.

programme.title: Widows
programme.desc: A police shootout leaves four thieves dead during an explosive armed robbery attempt in Chicago. Their indebted widows must now join forces to pull off their own heist and forge a future for themselves.

programme.title: On the Come Up
programme.desc: When her first hit song goes viral for all the wrong reasons, 16-year-old rapper Bri knows she has to make it in the music industry as she finds herself facing controversies and an eviction notice staring down her family.

programme.title: Broken Arrow
programme.desc: Set in Arizona in 1870, gold prospector Tom Jeffords is tired of the repetitive reprisal killings between settlers and the indigenous locals and plans a visit to the Apache leader, Cochise, to try and arrange a truce. After first being welcomed into the fold by Cochise, and even marrying the chief's daughter, old racial problems resurface.

programme.title: Gun Fury
programme.desc: Wild west tale about a rancher's search for a ruthless and violent outlaw who has kidnapped his fiancé.

programme.title: Gun Fury
programme.desc: Wild west tale about a rancher's search for a ruthless and violent outlaw who has kidnapped his fiancé.

programme.title: Siege of the Saxons
programme.desc: Upon the death of King Arthur, his daughter wins the right of selecting the knight she wants as a husband and heir.

programme.title: Cleopatra
programme.desc: Cleopatra uses both her political wiles and her body to first woo Julius Caesar and then Marc Antony to protect and even expand her realm. As history tells us, she was to meet an untimely end.

programme.title: Cleopatra
programme.desc: Cleopatra uses both her political wiles and her body to first woo Julius Caesar and then Marc Antony to protect and even expand her realm. As history tells us, she was to meet an untimely end.

programme.title: Anna
programme.desc: Beneath a Russian woman's striking beauty lies a hidden secret that will unleash her indelible strength and skill and lead to her becoming one of the most feared assassins on the planet.

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: Set several centuries in the future, the abandoned Alita is found in the scrapyard of Iron City by Ido, a compassionate cyber-doctor who takes the unconscious cyborg Alita to his clinic. When Alita awakens, she has no memory of who she is, nor does she have any recognition of the world she finds herself in. As Alita learns to navigate her new life and the treacherous streets of Iron City, Ido tries to shield her from her mysterious past.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: Terror by Night
programme.desc: When the legendary Star of Rhodesia diamond is stolen and a man is murdered on a train from London to Edinburgh, Sherlock Holmes attempts to discover which of the passengers is the culprit.

programme.title: Annie
programme.desc: Ever since her parents left her as a baby, little Annie has led a hard-knock life with her mean foster-mother, Miss Hannigan. However, that all changes when hard-nosed billionaire and mayor candidate Will Stacks takes her in on the recommendation of his advisers. Stacks believes that he's Annie's guardian angel, but the plucky youngster's confidence and sunny outlook may mean that Annie will save Will instead.

programme.title: Rosaline
programme.desc: A comedic retelling of Shakespeare's "Romeo and Juliet," told from the point of view of Romeo's jilted ex, Rosaline, the woman Romeo first claims to love before he falls for Juliet.

programme.title: Midway
programme.desc: On June 4, 1942, the Japanese navy plans a strike against American ships in the Pacific. For the next three days, the U.S. Navy and a squad of brave fighter pilots engage the enemy in one of the most important and decisive battles of World War II.

programme.title: Midway

programme.title: Daddy's Home
programme.desc: An insecure man competes for the affection of his stepchildren when their freewheeling father breezes back into town.

programme.title: Daredevil
programme.desc: Attorney Matt Murdock is blind, but his other four senses function with superhuman sharpness. By day, Murdock represents the downtrodden. At night, he is Daredevil, a masked vigilante, a relentless avenger of justice. When Wilson Fisk hires Bullseye to kill Daredevil, Murdock must rely on his own senses and search out the conspirators against justice, which may include his own girlfriend, Elektra.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy is excited to create an old-school wedding cake, tall, ornate and filled with live birds! A dad-to-be orders a lobster tail pastry for his wife.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' adventure begins on the Orkney islands, a place he's never been and is excited to explore. He's joined by top Scottish chef and good friend Nick Nairn. James gets straight to it and shops for hand-dived scallops, before setting up his mobile kitchen in Stromness Harbour to make BBQ scallops with black pudding, apple and hazelnut butter. James takes to the skies to visit North Ronaldsea where the people are outnumbered by sheep, and cooks up some lamb chops.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Fruits of the Sea
programme.desc: Rick prepares a fish tagine, monkfish tacos and some tangy Thai fishcakes.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Fruits of the Sea
programme.desc: Rick prepares squid in red wine and travels to Marseille to create the perfect bouillabaisse.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree has a variety of portable foods in-hand, with four fantastic on-the-go ideas that are perfect for teenage cowboys, colleagues, or just to have on standby in the freezer.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Beef skewers with greens and blue cheese mayo, shrimp and pineapple flatbreads with avocado cream, and all-in-one pesto chicken skillet supper. Plus, blueberry lemon ricotta bruschetta.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver creates deliciously gnarly recipes for under a quid a portion, from simple stuffed chicken, microwave chilli con carne and a pizza with no oven, to turning a humble pancake into a showstopper.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Roasted Vegetables
programme.desc: Ina, the queen of roasted vegetables, spills the beans on how to roast like a pro. Recipes include butternut squash hummus, roasted vegetable paella, roast potato and fennel soup, and Parmesan roasted cauliflower.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.desc: Crispy lemon Parmesan chicken; orecchiette with broccoli rabe and sausage; Parmesan chive smashed potatoes; celery and Parmesan salad.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is Bread Week, and the ten bakers have much to prove with three tough challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith. They must bake a fruity teatime Signature, a rather international-flavoured Technical, and an ambitious Showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is Dessert Week and judges have set three sticky challenges to test the remaining nine bakers. They must create a cracking meringue Signature, a wobbly Technical, and a complex chocolate Showstopper that promises to reveal all.

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: James is back in Brittany where he first learnt about French food as a student, and he is joined by fellow chef Martin Blunos for a dawn raid on the fish and vegetable market in Vannes.

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: James welcomes fellow chef Martin Blunos to his old student stamping ground in Brittany. They park up their trailer near Vannes, where the fresh seafood in the old market catches their imagination. Loaded up with a fabulous trawl they head back to the campsite to cook up Pot Au Feu and cod cheeks in local beer batter with home made tartare sauce.

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: James is back in Pornic, the Brittany seaside town where he learnt to be a French chef, and then as now the summer means a flood of delicious local strawberries.

programme.title: James Martin's French Road Trip
programme.desc: After shopping in a market in Saint-Servan, near St Malo, James prepares veal with rosemary and garlic, and cauliflower cooked with local cheese.

programme.desc: Rick prepares a fish tagine, monkfish tacos and some tangy Thai fishcakes.

programme.desc: It is Dessert Week and judges have set three sticky challenges to test the remaining nine bakers. They must create a cracking meringue Signature, a wobbly Technical, and a complex chocolate Showstopper that promises to reveal all.

programme.title: Ottolenghi's Mediterranean Feast
programme.desc: Yotam's journey begins with a trip to an unusual open-air restaurant - set in an idyllic cove and managed by a hermit. He grills red mullet, accompanied by a lemon and celery salad. Yotam also finds a Jewish restaurant that serves their twist on Tunisian dishes and where he prepares succulent chicken and sweetcorn meatballs, served with a tangy lemon salsa.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Adam is in Turin to taste the most iconic foods named after this city. He's tucking into a The Torinese - breaded beef cutlet, a veal and tuna dish called vitello tonnato alla Piemontese, and he's got a golden ticket to a chocolate factory.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Adam is in Genoa to taste the most iconic foods named after this city. He tucks into a pasta dish made with pesto Genevese, a fish stew called burrida, and a delicious focaccia. Plus, he visits the oldest sweet shop in Italy.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy's tasting it all. In Chicago, Illinois, a dive is refining small plate Mexican food with organic ingredients like shiitake mushroom empanadas and flautas with home-made queso fresco.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels across the United States to find the country's best traditional food, while undertaking tough eating challenges along the way.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a soul food spot in Washington, DC, a specialist hotdog place in Kansas City, and a seafood shack in Massachusetts.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Mediterranean spot in Tennessee, a cafe in Minnesota with wild rice French toast, and has turkey in Kentucky.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb makes the trip to Tampa for the ultimate Cuban platter at Florida's oldest restaurant, a stop at a speakeasy with a secret and 20 minutes of cookie combat with the "Cookie Munchers Challenge."

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb's palate-expanding pilgrimage takes him to Orlando for a hunger-slaying sandwich, a famous footlong and an 8.5 pound team challenge so big that he calls in backup from hometown hero and heartthrob Joey Fatone.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The team are like kids in a toy store literally, when they are asked to make an FAO Schwarz-themed cake. They also have a beach-themed cake to create.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The team has to make a traditional Chinese dragon cake and are busy preparing cannoli shells for the annual $25 Throw Back Cannoli Day.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Presenting the latest products live on television that can be ordered directly. The product range extends from vacuum cleaners and bath towels to sports equipment.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a pub in Sacramento, Calif., a Mexican-Salvadoran locale in Washington, D.C., and a vegan bar in Philadelphia.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy has Italian family recipes in New York, visits a Japanese grill turned diner in California, meets fans for burgers in Wisconsin.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a crepe joint in Colorado, a Jewish deli in New York, that serves kreplach and has lamb cheese steak in Washington.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a Cajun joint for smoked tasso, a Napoletana pizza place in San Antonio, Texas, and a Greek diner in Denver, Colorado.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Big Breakfast Buffet
programme.desc: Ina entertains her serious foodie friends Devon Fredericks and Eli Zabar with a breakfast bash. The pressure is on as Ina creates a buffet of potato pancetta frittata, overnight Belgian waffles and a fresh fruit platter.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.desc: It's a weekend of photos, fun and style as Ina's friend, photographer Miguel comes to visit. For his arrival, she makes spicy hermit bars, followed by the next day's lunch of courgette and goat cheese tart, and tomato carpaccio.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina shares her kitchen tips and professional strategies for chicken. Recipes include roasted chicken with radishes, chicken and spinach Waldorf salad, and truffled chicken liver mousse.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Weeknight Dinners
programme.desc: Ina's got weeknight dinners covered, pro-style! Recipes include roasted salmon tacos, cider roasted pork tenderloin and five-minute lemon capellini.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Fearless Food
programme.desc: Ina tackles daunting dishes with easy techniques, from a perfectly risen soufflé à l'orange to overcoming pastry fears with her totally professional-looking Anna's tomato tart. Plus, a foolproof caramel sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics
programme.desc: It's eggs all the way in an incredible master class taught by Ina. Recipes include Caesar salad with blue cheese and bacon, a pavlova fruit tart and eggs Benedict with an easy hollandaise sauce.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes root vegetables to another level, makes scrumptious sticky BBQ chicken, rattles out oozy cheesy enchiladas and turns things upside down with a pineapple cake, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a roast side of salmon with all the trimmings. Then, he turns his leftover salmon into a salmon filo pie.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: All the recipes in this episode cost under £1.90 per portion. Jamie cooks up a 'mothership' roast chicken with all the trimmings and turns the leftovers into chicken stew with dumplings.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef pal Nick Nairn to enjoy the luxury trip of a lifetime aboard the Royal Scotsman. James visits the kitchen aboard the train, where he cooks up a traditional duck a l'orange. Nick visits a special bakery housed in an old prisoner of war camp, and James uses the bread to make a tasty ham and cheese toastie in the grounds of historic Scone Palace. The day ends back aboard the Royal Scotsman, where James and Nick dine in style in the beautiful dining room.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a trip to the stunning Cairngorms National Park, where he's joined by chef pal Nick Nairn. Amongst their adventures, they visit some friendly reindeer and brave the rapids on the River Spey, where they go whitewater rafting. James takes a quad bike through the ancient pine forests, where he meets up with a farmer and his special breed of Highland cattle, which then inspires him to cook a tasty Highland beef carpaccio.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers are back in Scotland, but this time, they're in the beautiful region of Dumfries and Galloway. It's another place close to Dave and Si's hearts, a rural paradise with a glorious coastline which they used to visit.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers explore Lancashire. For Dave, the trip is an emotional homecoming, as he was born in the county. They start their trip in the glorious beauty of Morecambe Bay and head to the kitchen to make a dish dedicated to Lancashire.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: For their next adventure in the west of Britain, the Bikers are exploring Merseyside, a place that holds a lot of childhood memories for them both. It's also an area of the world renowned for its unbeatable musical heritage and cultural identity.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers continue their journey by heading to North West Wales, a remote but beautiful part of the UK. Alongside epic scenery, the Bikers discover a host of inspiring local producers that are celebrating the area's rich diversity.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes root vegetables to another level, makes scrumptious sticky BBQ chicken, rattles out oozy cheesy enchiladas and turns things upside down with a pineapple cake, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's in for some old school favourite and new school stunners. In Detroit, Michigan, a Polish joint that's been around for decades, servin' up old standards like Hungarian pancakes with goulash.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy finds a joint in Washington, with gooey burgers, a Southern/Jewish fusion spot in Philadelphia, and a Cajun spot in Louisiana.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy has a bacon blue cheeseburger in Sacramento, a meatball sandwich in Indianapolis, and turtle soup in Pennsylvania.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy tries homemade Italian in East Boston, stuffed burgers in Minnesota, and a great little diner in New Jersey.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a pub in Philadelphia for fried empanadas, a spot in Washington for stuffed pork chops, and a seafood joint in Maine.
